general.definition: Electronic Voting Machine - A device used for recording and counting votes in Indian elections.
general.extraFact: India was one of the first countries to implement EVMs nationwide.

general.definition: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail - A machine that prints a slip showing the candidate voted for.
general.extraFact: The slip is visible for 7 seconds before falling into a sealed box.

general.definition: None of the Above - A ballot option allowing voters to reject all candidates.
general.extraFact: NOTA votes do not affect the win/loss of the leading candidate.

general.definition: Elector Photo Identity Card - The official voter ID card issued by the ECI.
general.extraFact: It is not mandatory for voting if your name is in the roll and you have other ID.

general.definition: A specific geographical area represented by an elected official.

technical.definition: The brain of the EVM system, kept with the Presiding Officer.
technical.extraFact: It stores the final vote counts securely.

technical.definition: The part of the EVM where voters press the button for their candidate.
technical.extraFact: It contains the names and symbols of candidates.

technical.definition: A semi-permanent ink applied to a voter's finger to prevent duplicate voting.
technical.extraFact: It contains Silver Nitrate which reacts with sunlight to stain the skin.

technical.definition: The application form for new registration as a general voter.
technical.extraFact: Can be filled online via the Voter Service Portal.

technical.definition: Guidelines for political parties to ensure fair and peaceful elections.
technical.extraFact: It comes into force as soon as the ECI announces the election schedule.

misinformation.definition: AI-generated videos that impersonate real officials or candidates.
misinformation.extraFact: ECI monitors and removes deepfakes that aim to mislead voters.

misinformation.definition: Illegal practice of voting in the name of a person who is deceased or absent.
misinformation.extraFact: Strict verification by polling agents helps prevent this.

misinformation.definition: Propaganda or biased reports published as news in exchange for money.
misinformation.extraFact: ECI has strict committees to track and penalize paid news.
